name: Patout's Boiled Crawfish
recipeInstructions:
Alex Patout describes Crawfish as "a delicacy made for sharing--in fact, in Cajun country, boiling crawfish for only two people counts as a venial sin."
1. Wash the crawfish well and pick out any fish bones or other debris.
2. Fill a large stockpot about one-quarter full with water.
3. Add salt and peppers to the pot and bring to a boil.
4. Add whole onions, corn (in a cloth sack), and potatoes. Return to boil.
5. Cover, reduce heat to medium, and cook for 8 minutes.
6. Add crawfish; cover again and raise heat to high.
7. Cook until steam begins to escape from under the lid, about 7 more minutes.
8. Remove from heat and let sit for 4 minutes (do not remove the lid).
9. Remove onions, corn, and potatoes to a bowl and drain the crawfish.
10. Place crawfish in an insulated container (like a cooler or thick waterproof box).
11. Sprinkle salt, white pepper, red pepper over the crawfish and mix well to coat.
12. Cover and let sit for 7 minutes before serving.
Serve immediately with onions, corn, new potatoes, and plenty of French bread on a large table. When everyone is finished, those who are not "peeling for the house" can enjoy the tails in cold crawfish cocktail or salad, or use them for Fried Crawfish later.
Serves 8-10
Note: Most of the salt is added after cooking because too much salt during cooking causes the crawfish meat to adhere to the shell.
